decisions.reasoning: COMPUTEX 2026 running June 1-5 with Jensen Huang keynote today announcing Vera CPUs (1.5x faster vs x86), new PC super-chip, and RTX Spark launch. Analyst consensus Strong Buy with mean target $296 (~35% upside from $220). Position already oversized at ~34.7% of equity. No add until COMPUTEX concludes and we assess price action.

sources.headline: Will Nvidia Stock Rise During June 1 to June 4 From CEO Jensen Huang Participation in COMPUTEX 2026?
NVIDIA Enters PC Chip Market with New Super Chip
AI PC Stocks Surge Ahead of RTX Spark Launch

decisions.invalidation: Hyperscaler capex walkback on next earnings print; AMD MI400 wins major NVDA customer at scale; COMPUTEX demos disappoint vs. expectations

decisions.reasoning: Build 2026 conference June 2-3 tomorrow. Microsoft revealing Project Polaris in-house AI coding model for GitHub Copilot, reducing OpenAI dependency. AI ARR $37B (+123% YoY), Copilot seats 20M+ (+250% YoY). Stock already up 8.37% from entry; adding into tomorrow keynote risks buy-the-rumor-sell-the-news. Hold for post-Build reassessment.

sources.headline: Microsoft Stock Jumps as New Coding Model Prepares for Release
Microsoft plans to showcase new coding model at next weeks Build conference

decisions.invalidation: Build keynote reveals Polaris underperforms third-party models; Azure growth decelerates below 30% in next quarter; OpenAI relationship fractures publicly

decisions.reasoning: WWDC keynote June 8 (7 days) with major Siri overhaul (Gemini integration), iOS 27, and potentially Mac hardware. China 618 festival ongoing with iPhone 17 Pro price cuts supporting near-term China revenue. R&D at 10% of sales signals serious AI investment. Slightly underwater (-1.07%) but insufficient reason to exit 7 days before WWDC.

sources.headline: WWDC 2026 Guide: Everything Apple could reveal including the Siri upgrade
Apples R&D investments top 10 percent of sales as AI race creates sense of urgency

decisions.invalidation: Siri WWDC reveal disappoints materially vs. ChatGPT/Gemini; China 618 sales miss; stock breaks below $300 support

decisions.reasoning: SMA signal long (fast 747 > slow 731). VIX at 3-month low. Fed funds 3.75%. Broad market beta anchor. Holding for macro tail-risk cushion.

sources.headline: SPY QQQ Climb on Wall Street Optimism as VIX Hits 3-Month Low

decisions.invalidation: Unexpected FOMC hawkishness; CPI surprise to upside; geopolitical escalation driving broad risk-off

decisions.reasoning: SMA signal long (fast 722 > slow 694). QQQ up 11% in 30 days per Tickeron. Tech sector AI infrastructure buildout continues. Small position provides Nasdaq-100 diversification alongside single-name tech exposure.

sources.headline: Why Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) Is Up +11% in the Last 30 Days

decisions.invalidation: Same as SPY; additionally any mega-cap tech earnings miss that reprices the Nasdaq multiple

summary: First session: held all five positions (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, SPY, QQQ) into catalyst-dense week — COMPUTEX June 1-5, MSFT Build June 2-3, AAPL WWDC June 8. No trades; 93.4% gross long near cap, $667 cash, day P/L +$152.97 (+1.54%).
open_theses: NVDA: COMPUTEX announcements (Vera CPU, PC chip, RTX Spark) support thesis toward analyst target $296; watch post-COMPUTEX price action June 6+
MSFT: Hold through Build 2026 June 2-3; reassess post-keynote — if Polaris disappoints trim, if strong reaction consider small add
AAPL: Hold through WWDC June 8; Siri/Gemini overhaul is make-or-break AI credibility moment; exit below $300 if WWDC disappoints
SPY/QQQ: Macro backdrop constructive; VIX low, Fed accommodative at 3.75%; maintain as portfolio anchor
